title: ApprovedSupplier SD-JWT VC Schema
description: Schema for the Approved Supplier Attestation encoded as SD-JWT VC, based on the WE BUILD Attestation Rulebook for attestations of type Approved Supplier (Use Case SC5, eInvoicing) and draft-ietf-oauth-sd-jwt-vc

vct.description: Verifiable Credential Type — uniquely identifies the credential type. Always present in plain text. NOT selectively disclosable.

iss.description: Issuer identifier — a URI identifying the issuer of the credential (the Buyer's issuing system / EBW). As per RFC 7519. NOT selectively disclosable.

iat.description: Issued At — Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) indicating when the attestation was issued. NOT selectively disclosable.

exp.description: Expiration Time — Unix timestamp after which the attestation is no longer valid. NOT selectively disclosable.

nbf.description: Not Before — Unix timestamp before which the credential MUST NOT be accepted. Optional. NOT selectively disclosable.

sub.description: Subject — identifier for the subject of the credential (the approved Supplier legal entity). Optional. NOT selectively disclosable.

attestation_legal_category.description: Legal category of this attestation under eIDAS 2. For the Approved Supplier attestation this SHALL be 'EAA' (self-issued by the Buyer). NOT selectively disclosable.

schema_version.description: Version of the Approved Supplier attestation schema used. Optional metadata.

trust_anchor_url.description: URL at which a machine-readable version of the trust anchor used for verifying the attestation can be found or looked up. Optional metadata; for this EAA, trust is anchored in the issuer's EBWOID per Chapter 5 of the Rulebook.

cryptographically_bound_to.description: vct of the attestation on the same Wallet Unit to which this attestation is cryptographically bound (the Supplier's EBWOID). RECOMMENDED per ARB_28. NOT selectively disclosable.

approving_party.description: The Buyer (Peppol corner C4) issuing the approval. Mandatory. MAY be selectively disclosable.

legal_name.description: Registered legal name of the Buyer.

legal_id.description: Unique legal-person identifier (EUID) of the Buyer, sourced from the Buyer's EBWOID.

peppol_id.description: Peppol participant identifier of the Buyer (scheme:value, e.g. '0009:FR59120680088').

approved_supplier.description: The Supplier (Peppol corner C1) being approved — the holder/subject. Mandatory. MAY be selectively disclosable.

legal_name.description: Registered legal name of the approved Supplier.

legal_id.description: Unique legal-person identifier (EUID) of the Supplier, sourced from the Supplier's EBWOID.

peppol_id.description: Peppol participant identifier of the Supplier (scheme:value).

approval_scope.description: Scope of the approval per the code list in Section 2.8 of the Rulebook. Mandatory. MAY be selectively disclosable.

approval_valid_from.description: Date from which the approval is valid (RFC 3339 full-date). SHALL NOT be later than approval_valid_until when present. Mandatory. MAY be selectively disclosable.

approval_valid_until.description: Date until which the approval is valid (business validity, distinct from the technical exp claim). Optional. MAY be selectively disclosable.

approval_reference.description: Buyer-internal reference for the underlying commercial relationship (e.g. framework agreement number). Optional. MUST be selectively disclosable (SD claim).

status.description: Status object per SD-JWT VC status-list mechanism. MUST be present when validity period is greater than 24 hours.

type.description: Status mechanism type. For SD-JWT VC Status List this SHALL be 'status-list'.

status_list_credential.description: URI of the Status List Credential that contains the bitstring indicating revocation status.

status_list_index.description: Zero-based index into the status list bitstring for this credential.

status_purpose.description: Purpose of the status entry. For the Approved Supplier attestation this SHALL be 'revocation'.

cnf.description: Confirmation claims per SD-JWT VC. Holder binding via JWK to the Supplier's EBW is recommended.

jwk.description: Public key bound to the holder.
